Cabinet approves Rs 1.27 lakh crore Semicon 2.0 to strengthen India’s semiconductor ecosystem
The Union Cabinet, chaired by Prime Minister Narendra Modi, on Wednesday approved Semicon 2.0, a comprehensive programme with a budget outlay of ₹1,27,500 crore aimed at strengthening India’s semiconductor design and manufacturing ecosystem and providing long-term policy support to the sector.
By focusing on the entire semiconductor value chain, Semicon 2.0 aims to accelerate both semiconductor design and manufacturing in the country.
Highlighting the progress achieved under India Semiconductor Mission (ISM) 1.0, the government said 12 semiconductor manufacturing projects have so far been approved with a cumulative investment exceeding ₹1.64 lakh crore.

The projects are the development of a 43.218-kilometre link connecting NH-31 and the Varanasi Ring Road along the River Varuna to decongest Varanasi city in Uttar Pradesh.

The scheme tenure shall be 5 Years i.e. from FY 2026-27 to FY 2030-31.
The scheme provides incentive support on eligible sales for manufacturing of mobile phones in India at differentiated rates ranging from 2.25% to 5%.
The Scheme is also expected to generate around 60,000 direct jobs thereby contributing to economic growth, employment generation and strengthening India’s position in global electronics manufacturing hub.
Prime Minister’s Make in India vision has propelled electronics manufacturing to grow 7 times and exports to grow 11 times since FY 2014-15.
Mobile phone manufacturing has been the key driver of this growth and has emerged as the anchor of India’s electronics manufacturing ecosystem.
The tenure of PLI-LSEM ended on 31st March 2026.

The Union Cabinet, chaired by PM Modi, has approved the Sem 2.0 program with a budget outlay of Rs 1,27,500 crore.","score":0.8834878,"raw_content":null},{"url":"https://www.telegraphindia.com/amp/india/pm-modi-praises-cabinet-approvals-on-semiconductors-mobile-phones-urea/cid/2170235","title":"PM Modi praises Cabinet approvals on semiconductors, mobile phones, urea","content":"The Cabinet on Wednesday approved the Rs 1.27 lakh crore Semicon 2.0 programme to accelerate semiconductor design and manufacturing capabilities","score":0.6862234,"raw_content":null},{"url":"https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=eZIlOwFxnPA","title":"Government’s Commitment to Strengthen Semiconductor Ecosystem | 2 April 2026","content":"Government’s Commitment to Strengthen Semiconductor Ecosystem | 2 April 2026
NEWS ON AIR OFFICIAL

2 Apr 2026
Recently, Prime Minister Narendra Modi inaugurated the semiconductor facility in Gujarat, where he underlined the launch of India Semiconductor Mission 2.0, aimed at strengthening the country’s capabilities in chip manufacturing, design, and supply chains.

In this episode of Insights, we discuss the Government’s commitment to building a robust semiconductor ecosystem in India. The Semicon India Programme is driving the development of a complete value chain, from design to fabrication, assembly, testing, and packaging- positioning India as a global manufacturing hub.

The Government has approved 10 semiconductor projects with investments of around ₹1.6 lakh crore, with several units already under construction and some beginning commercial production.

Major projects include facilities by companies such as Micron Technology, Tata Electronics, and CG Power and Industrial Solutions, along with the Kaynes Technology unit. These projects span multiple states and cover a wide range of semiconductor manufacturing and packaging technologies.

The initiative is expected to generate significant employment opportunities, with the broader electronics manufacturing sector already supporting around 25 lakh jobs. Semiconductor manufacturing, as a foundational industry, is also set to create extensive indirect employment across the supply chain.

The programme further highlights India’s strategic push towards self-reliance under the vision of Atmanirbhar Bharat, while strengthening its position in the global semiconductor value chain.

President of India Electronics and Semiconductor Association (IESA) Ashok Chandak shared his views on this important topic in this program, the program was moderated by Arjun J Chaudhuri.
